title: Add per-market funding-rate cap
summary: Read the keeper entry point first, then the helper it now delegates clamping to, then where the cap is stored, and finally the test. The cap is the whole change; everything else supports it.

steps.title: Entry point: settleFunding() now clamps the rate
steps.why: settleFunding() now clamps the epoch rate before applying it.

beats.text: Start here: this is the function the keeper calls each epoch.
The clamp is the core behavior, and it needs to happen before the rate is applied.
When you review this, check that the balance mutation still happens after the capped value is chosen.

steps.title: The helper it delegates to: _capRate() (new)
steps.why: _capRate() owns the boundary rule that settleFunding() now delegates.

beats.text: settleFunding() hands the clamping off here so the entry point stays about flow.
This require is the safety hinge for the unchecked math below.
Now check that the returned value is capped without changing under-limit rates.

steps.title: Where the cap is read from: marketConfig
steps.why: marketConfig is unchanged context for the per-market cap source.

beats.text: Unchanged, but you need to see it: _capRate() reads fundingRateCap from this struct.
Because this is per-market storage, each market can set its own ceiling.

steps.title: Test: cap is enforced at the boundary
steps.why: The funding test proves over-limit, under-limit, and boundary behavior.

beats.text: Final proof: this test confirms the cap clamps an over-limit rate.
It also leaves an under-limit rate untouched, which protects the normal path.
Pause on the rate-equals-cap case because that is the off-by-one risk in _capRate().
